title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Compiler Case: Request Reply Boundary
summary: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request reply boundary increment in pattern selection and coupling decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Request Reply Boundary · Theory Request Reply Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9427 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
